evidence: AEP invested $100 million for a 10% ownership interest in the common stock [of GWAI] and received a warrant for the option to acquire an additional 5% of GWAI s common stock for $50 million.
Contingent upon GWAI s achievement of defined performance-based milestones, AEP will invest up to an additional $100 million for up to an additional 10% of GWAI s common stock.
In January 2026, AEP made an additional $25 million investment for an incremental 2.5% interest in GWAI s common stock.
GWAI is focused on developing AI-centric operating systems and applications that optimize utility operations and infrastructure.

AEP Management determined that previously selected technology for an in-process software development project... was no longer probable of being completed... due to expanded use of, and potential capabilities for, AI-centric software.
Gigawatt AI Inc., an equity interest joint venture formed to build the AI-centric operating system for utilities.
Information technology failure, including failure of AI technology, that impairs AEP s information technology infrastructure or disrupts normal business operations.
The rapid evolution and increased adoption of AI technologies may intensify AEP s cybersecurity risks... impairing the availability of electricity transmitted and distributed by AEP.

Advancements in artificial intelligence and other emerging technologies may require significant changes to the size, skills, and composition of AEP s workforce... adversely affect AEP s operations and financial performance.

capex_mentions.quote: AEP invested $100 million for a 10% ownership interest in the common stock [of GWAI]
capex_mentions.context: August 2025 equity investment in Gigawatt AI, an AI-centric operating system developer for utilities

capex_mentions.quote: warrant for the option to acquire an additional 5% of GWAI s common stock for $50 million
capex_mentions.context: Warrant associated with August 2025 Gigawatt AI investment

capex_mentions.quote: AEP will invest up to an additional $100 million for up to an additional 10% of GWAI s common stock
capex_mentions.context: Contingent milestone-based additional investment in Gigawatt AI

capex_mentions.quote: AEP made an additional $25 million investment for an incremental 2.5% interest in GWAI s common stock
capex_mentions.context: January 2026 follow-on investment in Gigawatt AI upon milestone achievement

limitations: AI narrative concreteness scored 4 rather than 5 because no quantified business results (e.g., cost savings, efficiency metrics) from AI deployments are disclosed; the Gigawatt AI investment is described as in-progress with milestones, not yet producing measurable outcomes.

The $66 million internal-use software impairment related to AI-centric software strategy is a write-down, not a forward investment, and was excluded from capex_mentions but is relevant context for AI strategy.
Excerpts do not include the full risk factors section; however, multiple [risk_factors] labeled excerpts are present and were used for scoring ai_risk_disclosure_depth.

Large load/data center tariff and infrastructure build-out capex is discussed but not broken out as AI-attributable spend distinct from general load growth capex; only the Gigawatt AI equity investments are clearly AI-attributable.
